17: use anyhow::Result;
18: use clap::Parser;
19: use cli::{Cli, Commands, ConfigCommands, ScopeCommands, SkillCommands, WorktreeCommands};
20: use std::time::Instant;

22: fn exit_code_for(e: &anyhow::Error) -> i32 {
23:     use crate::error::EzError;
24:     if let Some(ez) = e.downcast_ref::<EzError>() {
25:         match ez {
26:             EzError::GhError(_) => 2,
27:             EzError::RebaseConflict(_) => 3,
28:             EzError::StaleRemoteRef(_) => 4,
29:             EzError::OnTrunk
30:             | EzError::BranchNotInStack(_)
31:             | EzError::AlreadyAtTop
32:             | EzError::AlreadyAtBottom
33:             | EzError::BranchAlreadyExists(_)
34:             | EzError::AlreadyInitialized
35:             | EzError::NotInitialized
36:             | EzError::UserMessage(_) => 5,
37:             EzError::NothingToCommit | EzError::UnstagedChanges => 6,
38:             _ => 1,
39:         }
40:     } else {
41:         1
42:     }
43: }
44: 
45: fn main() {
46:     let start = Instant::now();
47: 
48:     // Use try_parse so clap errors go through our formatting instead of raw stderr.
49:     let cli = match Cli::try_parse() {
50:         Ok(cli) => cli,
51:         Err(e) => {
52:             handle_clap_error(e, start);
53:             return;
54:         }
55:     };
56: 
57:     // Check if this is a command that should show the first-run hint.
58:     let is_setup_command = matches!(cli.command, Commands::Setup { .. } | Commands::ShellInit);
59: 
60:     match run(cli) {
61:         Ok(()) => {
62:             // First-run hint: prompt to run `ez setup` once.
63:             if !is_setup_command && !cmd::setup::is_setup_done() {
64:                 ui::hint("Shell not configured — run `ez setup` for PATH and worktree auto-cd");
65:             }
66:             ui::exit_status(0, start.elapsed());
67:         }
68:         Err(e) => {
69:             let code = exit_code_for(&e);
70:             ui::error(&format!("{e:#}"));
71:             ui::exit_status(code, start.elapsed());
72:             std::process::exit(code);
73:         }
74:     }
75: }
76: 
77: fn handle_clap_error(e: clap::Error, start: Instant) {
78:     use clap::error::ErrorKind;
79: 
80:     match e.kind() {
81:         // Help, version, and bare `ez` / `ez worktree`: show help, exit 0.
82:         // These are Level 0/1 discovery — agents call them to learn available commands.
83:         ErrorKind::DisplayHelp
84:         | ErrorKind::DisplayVersion
85:         | ErrorKind::DisplayHelpOnMissingArgumentOrSubcommand => {
86:             print!("{e}");
87:             ui::exit_status(0, start.elapsed());
88:         }
89:         // Missing required arg: rewrite as ez-style hint.
90:         ErrorKind::MissingRequiredArgument => {
91:             ui::error(&format!("{e}"));
92:             ui::hint("Run `ez <command> --help` for usage details");
93:             ui::exit_status(5, start.elapsed());
94:             std::process::exit(5);
95:         }
96:         // Unknown subcommand or invalid arg.
97:         ErrorKind::InvalidSubcommand | ErrorKind::UnknownArgument => {
98:             ui::error(&format!("{e}"));
99:             ui::hint("Run `ez --help` to see available commands");
100:             ui::exit_status(5, start.elapsed());
101:             std::process::exit(5);
102:         }
103:         // Everything else: print clap's message with our exit line.
104:         _ => {
105:             ui::error(&format!("{e}"));
106:             ui::exit_status(5, start.elapsed());
107:             std::process::exit(5);
108:         }
109:     }
110: }

112: fn run(cli: Cli) -> Result<()> {
113:     match cli.command {
114:         Commands::Init { trunk } => cmd::init::run(trunk),
115:         Commands::Create {
116:             name,
117:             message,
118:             all,
119:             all_files,
120:             from,
121:             no_worktree,
122:             scope,
123:             scope_mode,
124:             hook,
125:         } => cmd::create::run(
126:             &name,
127:             message.as_deref(),
128:             all,
129:             all_files,
130:             from.as_deref(),
131:             no_worktree,
132:             &scope,
133:             scope_mode,
134:             hook.as_deref(),
135:         ),
136:         Commands::Commit {
137:             message,
138:             all,
139:             all_files,
140:             if_changed,
141:             paths,
142:         } => {
143:             // Join repeated -m flags with double newline (matching git behavior).
144:             let full_message = message.join("\n\n");
145:             cmd::commit::run(&full_message, all, all_files, if_changed, &paths)
146:         }
147:         Commands::Amend { message, all } => cmd::amend::run(message.as_deref(), all),
148:         Commands::Push {
149:             draft,
150:             no_draft,
151:             no_pr,
152:             title,
153:             body,
154:             body_file,
155:             base,
156:             stack,
157:             stage_all,
158:             stage_all_files,
159:             message,
160:             repo,
161:             remote,
162:         } => cmd::push::run(
163:             draft,
164:             no_draft,
165:             no_pr,
166:             title.as_deref(),
167:             body.as_deref(),
168:             body_file.as_deref(),
169:             base.as_deref(),
170:             stack,
171:             stage_all,
172:             stage_all_files,
173:             message.as_deref(),
174:             repo.as_deref(),
175:             remote.as_deref(),
176:         ),
177:         Commands::Submit {
178:             draft,
179:             no_draft,
180:             title,
181:             body,
182:             body_file,
183:             repo,
184:             remote,
185:         } => cmd::submit::run(
186:             draft,
187:             no_draft,
188:             title.as_deref(),
189:             body.as_deref(),
190:             body_file.as_deref(),
191:             repo.as_deref(),
192:             remote.as_deref(),
193:         ),
194:         Commands::Sync {
195:             dry_run,
196:             autostash,
197:             force,
198:         } => cmd::sync::run(dry_run, autostash, force),
199:         Commands::Restack { force } => cmd::restack::run(force),
200:         Commands::Up => cmd::navigate::up(),
201:         Commands::Down => cmd::navigate::down(),
202:         Commands::Top => cmd::navigate::top(),
203:         Commands::Bottom => cmd::navigate::bottom(),
204:         Commands::Switch { name, no_cd_required } => cmd::checkout::run(name.as_deref(), no_cd_required),
205:         Commands::List { json } => cmd::list::run(json),
206:         Commands::Log { json } => cmd::log::run(json),
207:         Commands::Status { json } => cmd::status::run(json),
208:         Commands::Diff { stat, name_only } => cmd::diff::run(stat, name_only),
209:         Commands::Parent => cmd::parent::run(),
210:         Commands::Delete { branch, force, yes } => cmd::delete::run(branch.as_deref(), force, yes),
211:         Commands::Move { onto, force } => cmd::move_branch::run(&onto, force),
212:         Commands::Merge { method, yes, stack, local, strategy, into, force } => cmd::merge::run(&method, yes, stack, local, &strategy, into.as_deref(), force),
213:         Commands::Fold { range, name } => cmd::fold::run(&range, name.as_deref()),
214:         Commands::PrEdit {
215:             title,
216:             body,
217:             body_file,
218:         } => cmd::pr_edit::run(title.as_deref(), body.as_deref(), body_file.as_deref()),
219:         Commands::Draft => cmd::draft::run(false),
220:         Commands::Ready => cmd::draft::run(true),
221:         Commands::PrLink => cmd::pr_link::run(),
222:         Commands::Pr => cmd::pr_view::run(),
223:         Commands::Update { version, check } => cmd::update::run(version.as_deref(), check),
224:         Commands::Setup { yes } => cmd::setup::run(yes),
225:         Commands::Scope(args) => match args.command {
226:             ScopeCommands::Show => cmd::scope::show(),
227:             ScopeCommands::Add { mode, patterns } => cmd::scope::add(&patterns, mode),
228:             ScopeCommands::Set { mode, patterns } => cmd::scope::set(&patterns, mode),
229:             ScopeCommands::Clear => cmd::scope::clear(),
230:         },
231:         Commands::Skill(args) => match args.command {
232:             SkillCommands::Install => cmd::skill::install(),
233:             SkillCommands::Uninstall => cmd::skill::uninstall(),
234:         },
235:         Commands::ShellInit => cmd::shell_init::run(),
236:         Commands::Fork { remote, from } => cmd::fork::run(remote.as_deref(), from.as_deref()),
237:         Commands::Config(args) => match args.command {
238:             ConfigCommands::List => cmd::config::list(),
239:             ConfigCommands::Get { key, branch } => cmd::config::get(&key, branch.as_deref()),
240:             ConfigCommands::Set { key, value, branch } => {
241:                 cmd::config::set(&key, &value, branch.as_deref())
242:             }
243:         },
244:         Commands::Worktree(args) => match args.command {
245:             WorktreeCommands::Create { name, from } => {
246:                 cmd::worktree::create(&name, from.as_deref())
247:             }
248:             WorktreeCommands::Delete { name, force, yes } => {
249:                 cmd::delete::run(Some(&name), force, yes)
250:             }
251:             WorktreeCommands::List => cmd::list::run(false),
252:         },
253:     }
254: }

256: #[cfg(test)]
257: mod tests {
258:     use super::*;
259:     use crate::error::EzError;
260: 
261:     #[test]
262:     fn test_exit_codes() {
263:         assert_eq!(
264:             exit_code_for(&EzError::RebaseConflict("x".into()).into()),
265:             3
266:         );
267:         assert_eq!(
268:             exit_code_for(&EzError::StaleRemoteRef("x".into()).into()),
269:             4
270:         );
271:         assert_eq!(exit_code_for(&EzError::OnTrunk.into()), 5);
272:         assert_eq!(exit_code_for(&EzError::GhError("x".into()).into()), 2);
273:         assert_eq!(exit_code_for(&EzError::NothingToCommit.into()), 6);
274:         assert_eq!(exit_code_for(&EzError::UnstagedChanges.into()), 6);
275:         assert_eq!(exit_code_for(&anyhow::anyhow!("generic")), 1);
276:     }
277: }
